content:
Financial trail analysis reveals:

- ₹4.2 crore transferred from VedaCorp (A/C: SBIN-7890123456) to Sparrow Holdings (A/C: ICICI-5544332211) on Feb 15, 2022, under invoice #VSP-2022-001. No service delivery evidence found.
- ₹7.5 crore transferred from Sparrow Holdings to Vireo Ventures (A/C: HDFC-1122334455) on July 12, 2022.
- ₹6.8 crore transferred from Vireo Ventures to Mauritius account (MUS8765432109876543210) on July 28, 2022.
- ₹6.0 crore withdrawn from Mauritius account on August 15, 2022, via wire transfer to 'A. Mehta' in Panaji, Goa — used to purchase Plot No. 7B, Candolim Beach, registered under 'A. Mehta' (PAN: APMCM7890R).

PAN APMCM7890R is not registered to Amit Mehta (Dr. Nair’s brother) or any known individual. It was issued to a fictitious entity 'A. Mehta Enterprises' registered via a shell agent in Goa. The property is now under mortgage to an NBFC called 'Shree Capital Finance' — whose owner is linked to Arjun Mehta via a nominee director.

Sunita Rao’s ₹25 lakh deposit on July 18, 2022, originated from Sparrow Holdings via a chain of 3 intermediary accounts. The source: funds from VedaCorp.

Conclusion: ₹18.7 crore was systematically siphoned. The Goa property is the ultimate beneficiary asset. Arjun Mehta is the central node in the financial network.
